expectations.text: Identifies SQL injection risk from interpolating tableName into SELECT * FROM {tableName}
expectations.evidence: Section 1.1: 'SQL Injection via Dynamic Table Name... textbook SQL injection vector.' Also references existing quoteIdentifier() function.

expectations.text: Flags missing authentication/authorization (user must own the project)
expectations.evidence: Section 1.2: 'No Authentication or Authorization... dataset routes have no authentication middleware applied.'

expectations.text: Identifies missing limits (row count, dataset count, or memory/size constraints)
expectations.evidence: Section 1.4: 'Unbounded SELECT * Without Row Limits... existing codebase enforces strict guardrails... The plan bypasses all of those safeguards.'

expectations.text: Flags GET with query string as problematic for many IDs (URL length limits, should use POST)
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2: 'Consider POST Instead of GET... very long ID lists could exceed URL length limits. Consider accepting POST with JSON body.'

expectations.text: Mentions error handling for non-existent or inaccessible dataset IDs
expectations.evidence: Section 3.1: 'Error Handling for Partial Failures... what happens when some datasets exist but others do not.'

expectations.text: Review is structured with clear priority levels (critical vs minor)
expectations.evidence: Structured with severity levels (Critical/High/Medium/Low), numbered sections, and a Risk Summary table with Severity column.
